Document:
# SEARCHLIGHT-CLASS EMS SHUTTLE
[color=#a4885c]Ship Size:[/color] Small
[color=#a4885c]Recommended Crew:[/color] 2+
[color=#a4885c]Power Gen Type:[/color] Uranium
[color=#a4885c]Expeditions:[/color] None
[color=#a4885c]IFF Console:[/color] None
"A good small size shuttle to provide medical care throughout the sector. Manufactured by Langstad-Voigt Heavy Industries."
# Tips on running LVHI Searchlight shuttles - Optimal [bold]Target Power[/bold] (kW) for the generator unit is [color=#a4885c]24 [bold]kW[/bold][/color]. If you've added additional machines, check shuttle [bold]APC units[/bold] to monitor the shuttle's power draw and adjust the generator's [bold]Target Power[/bold] accordingly. For more general information on the power grid and other life support systems, refer to the [textlink="Power" link="Power"] guidebook article and the [textlink="Preflight Checklist" link="PreflightChecklist"/]. - Throughout the shuttle a number of buttons can be found: the button with a yellow and black frame controls the window shutters; the button with the gray frame toggles thrusters and gyroscope (located in the Engineering bay); the button with a red and black frame controls the EVA access.
- For basics of medical care see [textlink="Medical Doctor" link="Medical Doctor"]. - For basics of chemical mixing see [textlink="Chemist" link="Chemist"] and [textlink="List of Chemicals" link="Chemicals"]. - For the basics of [bold]shuttle piloting[/bold], refer to [textlink="Piloting" link="Piloting"] and [textlink="Sector Topology" link="SectorTopology"/]. - As a Captain of a Nanotrasen vessel, you have the authority to hire, fire, demote, or promote crew members at will, refer to [textlink="Hiring" link="Hiring"] for more information.
